title: allophylus ternatus
intro: Allophylus ternatus, also known as the West African apricot or African apricot, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and seeds, which have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Allophylus ternatus include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Allophylus ternatus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its seeds can be used to make a medicinal oil, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
